subject: Geology
title: Alaska 1964
description: A fast breakdown of Alaska 1964 for builders.

hook.text: On Good Friday 1964, a magnitude 9.2 quake struck Alaska. The ground in Anchorage dropped 2.4 meters in seconds.

buildup.text: The Great Alaska Earthquake was the second largest ever recorded. It ruptured along 800 km of the subduction zone.

discovery.text: Landslides destroyed Anchorage neighborhoods. A tsunami devastated coastal towns and reached as far as California.

twist.text: Only 131 people died because Alaska was sparsely populated. A quake this size near a major city would be catastrophic.

climax.text: The Alaska quake advanced our understanding of subduction zones and the link between megathrust quakes and tsunamis.

punchline.text: Low population saved Alaska from far worse.

quiz.question: What was significant about the 1964 Alaska earthquake?
options: It was the first earthquake ever recorded
It was the second largest recorded and advanced subduction zone science
